title: Sunrise City
song_notes: Bright synth stabs and a chorus built for convertibles. Designed for the first ten minutes of a road trip, when optimism still outruns the gas gauge.

title: Midnight Coding
song_notes: Filtered rainfall over a muted kick drum. Makes 2am feel like a warm library instead of an empty apartment.

title: Storm Runner
song_notes: Driving guitars with no quiet moments. A straight shot of adrenaline for workouts where you'd rather not think.

title: Library Rain
song_notes: Jazz piano samples under a fireplace crackle. Asks for attention but never demands it.

title: Gym Hero
song_notes: Peak-hour treadmill fuel. Sidechain compression so heavy you can feel it in your molars.

title: Spacewalk Thoughts
song_notes: Slow drifting pads with no percussion. For long-form focus or when anxiety is asking you to lie flat on the floor.

title: Coffee Shop Stories
song_notes: Upright bass and brushed drums. Makes a rainy Sunday feel chosen instead of endured.

title: Night Drive Loop
song_notes: Analog synths and tail-light reds. Built for 11pm freeway stretches where your thoughts get a little too loud.

title: Focus Flow
song_notes: Metronomic low-valence lofi with no vocal samples. Disappears into the background and lets deep work happen.

title: Rooftop Lights
song_notes: Jangly guitars and festival chants. The feeling of arriving somewhere you've been looking forward to.

title: Golden Hour Hustle
song_notes: Trap hi-hats and brass stabs. Designed for the walk to the pitch meeting not the meeting itself.

title: Moonlit Sonata
song_notes: Solo piano in a minor key. Restraint rather than sadness — every note could have been louder but wasn't.

title: Velvet Sunrise
song_notes: Rhodes piano and slow-burn vocals. More late-night tenderness than Saturday-night seduction.

title: Iron Collapse
song_notes: Double-kick drums and detuned guitars. Genuinely aggressive — skip if you're already upset.

title: Porch Light Song
song_notes: Acoustic guitar and a voice that sounds like it's been smoking since college. For flipping through old photos.

title: Drop Zone
song_notes: Festival-main-stage drops designed for collective catharsis. Euphoria engineered not accidental.

title: Empty Chairs
song_notes: Spare arrangement with a vocal that cracks at exactly the right word. Sadness that feels earned rather than performed.

title: Island Drift
song_notes: Offbeat guitar skanks and steel drums. The musical equivalent of someone handing you a cold drink.
